idea.title: Ice Fracture Foreman
idea.hook: Tap a frozen lake to crack it in branching fissures, isolating fish into a shrinking pond before the cracks reach your hut.
idea.hook_ru: Касанием раскалывай замёрзшее озеро ветвящимися трещинами, загоняй рыбу в уменьшающийся пруд, пока трещины не дошли до избы.
idea.prototype: Top-down 2D canvas grid of hex ice tiles. Each tap propagates a 3-5 tile branching crack toward the nearest stress point. Fish swim only on intact tiles; corral them into a 7-hex wooden pen in the top-left by carving fractures as walls. Score = fish caught. Lose when fracture network touches the central hut hex or its 6 neighbours. Daily seed for stress-point layout. Mouse/touch only.
idea.mutation_of: Mutates 18_ice_cleanup — the ice-as-medium frame stays, but the verb changes from 'clean' to 'corral'. Where ice_cleanup was a roller-skim score game and the existing frost_crack/glacier_splitter were shatter-score and split-line games respectively, this is a spatial-puzzle: cracks are walls, not damage. The branching fracture propagation pulled from nobice/2350 'procedural ice destruction is a strong browser-game toy' signal. Hex-tile chunking sidesteps mesh-fracture cost while keeping the satisfying readable break.
idea.why_novel: First game in the portfolio where ice destruction is a corraling tool rather than a scoring goal. Hex-grid + fish-silhouette-through-ice visual identity is distinct from prior ice games. Stress-point AI for crack propagation means every tap is a decision about where the fissure will land, not just an animation. Pen-vs-hut spatial puzzle uses position scarcity as non-timer pressure.
idea.non_timer_pressure: Two stacking non-timer pressures: (1) intact-tile scarcity — every crack permanently removes ice tiles fish can swim on; (2) crack-spread budget — CRACK_BUDGET_BASE = 28 propagation tiles per round, decremented per crack, no countdown clock.
idea.lose_condition: Any crack tile reaches the hut hex at board centre or one of its 6 immediate neighbours (the hut footprint). gs = 'GAMEOVER' immediately on commit.

sources.title: Ice Fracture Foreman
sources.snippet: Entry #1 — Ice destruction flagged as strong browser-game toy. Hex-tile chunking sidesteps the mesh-fracture cost while keeping the satisfying readable break. Distinct from prior ice games — this is corral/spatial puzzle with branching propagation.

sources.title: Screenshot Saturday + procedural ice
sources.snippet: Screenshot Saturday community signal called out procedural ice destruction as a strong browser-game toy across multiple devlogs this week.

sources.title: Weekly Gaming News: Marble Sort Voodoo $3.7M
sources.snippet: Weekly gaming news roundup. Cited as supporting signal for casual mechanic prototyping for Tier-1 markets.

synthesizer_brief: Hex-grid ice lake with tap-to-fissure mechanic. Each tap fires a 3-5 tile branching crack toward the nearest of 4 daily-seeded stress points. Fish auto-swim hex-to-hex with 40% bias toward the top-left pen; cracks block their movement. Catch target fish (3 → 6 across 4 rounds) before crack budget zeroes or a fissure touches the central hut. Mutates 18_ice_cleanup's ice-as-medium frame into a spatial-puzzle corral game. Stress-point AI makes the same tap fire different paths from different starting hexes.

alternatives.title: Shader Saturday
alternatives.hook: Push render passes — fog, bloom, chromatic aberration, scanlines — onto a static cube until the screenshot scores high enough to post in #screenshotsaturday.
alternatives.why_novel: Self-referential shader-stacking game riding live Screenshot Saturday cultural moment + Russian gamedev meme.

alternatives.title: Token Throttle
alternatives.hook: You're a dispatcher routing tasks to cheap or expensive AI agents — pick wrong and your monthly budget burns before quota refills.
alternatives.why_novel: Tiered Haiku/Sonnet/Opus routing with rework-loop penalty on wrong-tier assignments.

alternatives.title: Tier-1 Pitch
alternatives.hook: Drag elevator-pitch cards into a 30-second window and beat the AI buyer panel before they swipe left.
alternatives.why_novel: Self-referential about Tim's own portfolio mistake (pivot before shipping for the world).

alternatives.title: Tiny Markov Dunge
alternatives.hook: A 200-byte room-generator paints your dungeon in real time — your only weapon is editing the seed before the boss spawns from your own typo.
alternatives.why_novel: Live PCG visualisation as gameplay; edit-the-seed verb is novel.
